exemplar.title: To what extent do the sewage management, environmental quality and waste collection services differ in formal and informal urban areas in Planning Area 2.1 of Rio de Janeiro?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do the sewage management, environmental quality and waste collection services differ in formal and informal urban areas in Planning Area 2.1 of Rio de Janeiro?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a clear, consistent spatial and thematic focus.
The research question is clearly stated, sharply focused, and operationalized into measurable hypotheses.
Methodology is comprehensive, blending primary transect surveys, interviews, GIS mapping and secondary data sources, and is documented with appropriate detail.
weaknesses: Minor digressions in the background section could be more tightly linked to the research focus.
Some methodological details (e.g., transect randomization, Google Maps sampling frame) require further clarification for full repeatability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student has excelled in clearly delimiting the study area and thematic scope, formulating a sharply focused research question, and deploying an appropriately varied methodological approach. Although there are a few small lapses in background specificity and sampling detail, these do not detract from an otherwise flawless Focus and Method performance meriting the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Subject knowledge is thorough, demonstrating excellent engagement with urban-geographical concepts, official statistics and theoretical frameworks.
Discipline-specific terminology (e.g., âvector-borne disease,â âbiodigester,â âsubmarine outfallâ) is used accurately and consistently throughout.
Sources are effectively integrated (GIS, WHO/SDG standards, census data) to underpin explanations and contextualize findings.
weaknesses: Some data (e.g., 2010 census figures, WHO recommendations) are uncritically accepted and would benefit from discussion of their local applicability or currency.
A few bibliographic references are incomplete or lack full citation details.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the essay displays an excellent command of subject-specific knowledge and consistently precise use of terminology. Minor lapses concerning source currency and citation completeness do not undermine the very high level of Knowledge and Understanding demonstrated, justifying the full 6 marks.

strengths: Research application is consistently relevant and insightful, with primary and secondary data effectively mobilized to address each hypothesis.
Analysis is well supported by quantitative results, GIS overlays and qualitative observations, leading to logical, evidence-based conclusions.
The argument is coherent, well-structured around the three hypotheses, with counter-arguments and methodological caveats acknowledged to strengthen balance.
weaknesses: Some evaluative claims (e.g., health impacts) extend beyond the data and would benefit from direct evidence or inferential statistics.
Synthesis in the conclusion could more critically weigh alternative explanations (topographical vs. socioeconomic drivers of disparity).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs Critical Thinking is of an exceptional standard: research is applied with precision, analyses are underpinned by robust evidence, and the argument remains focused and coherent throughout. Although there are a few overreaching inferences and opportunities for deeper critical synthesis, the performance fully satisfies the highest band and warrants all 12 marks.

strengths: The structure is exemplary, with a clear title page, abstract, contents, logically sequenced chapters and seamless integration of figures and tables.
Layout considerations (word count, pagination, consistent captions, formatting) enhance readability and navigation.
Headings and subheadings mirror the research framework, guiding the reader through each hypothesis and conclusion.
weaknesses: A handful of figures have minor caption or legend omissions (e.g., map projections, negativeâpositive scales) that could be tightened.
Occasional graphic overcrowding and table breaks slightly interrupt flow but do not impede comprehension.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is of the highest quality: the report adheres meticulously to IB conventions, with an intuitive structure and a clean, professional layout. Minor graphical and formatting imperfections are too slight to detract from full marks in this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Research evidenceâboth primary (surveys, transects, interviews) and secondary (official statistics, GIS layers)âis **consistently mobilised to answer the RQ**, showing clear relevance to each hypothesis. Examples include overlaying sewage network maps with favela boundaries and converting green-space area into per-capita metrics.  
The application is mostly insightful, yet limited primary coverage of Rocinha and time-specific bias in Vidigal weaken universality of findings. The student mitigates by triangulating with secondary data and acknowledging limitations, indicating reflective application of research.  
Overall the strand reflects good to very good application, fitting early in the 7-9 band; a score of 8 is appropriate.

The methodology blends **multiple primary techniques (transect surveys, structured environmental/waste audits, two interviews, GIS mapping) with secondary statistical datasets** from government sources. Sampling strategy (ten 100 m transects per neighbourhood) and timing are explained, and limitations such as safety constraints in favelas are acknowledged.  
However, the exclusion of Rocinha from primary work and dependence on opportunistic data collection in Vidigal reduce completeness; some methods (e.g., tree counts from Google imagery) raise validity concerns that are only briefly addressed. Overall, the approach is mostly appropriate and sufficiently documented, placing the work securely in the 3-4 band at the upper end (4).

Throughout the essay the student demonstrates **sound grasp of urban-geographical concepts** (formal/informal settlements, infrastructure provision, SDGs, spatial inequality) and supports explanations with relevant literature, government data, and field evidence.  
Use of GIS maps, SDG frameworks and WHO standards indicates engagement with authoritative sources, though some data (e.g., 2010 census figures) are dated and occasionally accepted uncritically. Depth of theoretical discussion (e.g., urban political ecology or spatial justice) could have been richer.  
Consequently, knowledge is clear and generally accurate but not consistently sophisticated, aligning with the upper part of the 3-4 band (score 4).

Figures are plentiful, numbered and captioned; tables are formatted; word count, title and page numbers are provided. **Most visual material is legible and appropriately referenced, and the overall presentation is neat and reader-friendly.**  
Some graphics are pixelated or carry excessive annotation that crowds the page, and a few tables run across page breaks awkwardly, but these are minor lapses. Citation style is generally consistent, though occasional broken URLs appear.  
Given that layout conventions are applied correctly with only small imperfections, the work earns a solid 3 within the top band.

The student employs **discipline-specific terminology confidently and accurately**, e.g., âsubmarine outfallâ, âvector-borne diseaseâ, âbiodigesterâ, âstorm-water network contaminationâ, and uses SDG numbering correctly. Key concepts such as accessibility, density, and environmental quality metrics are applied consistently across data collection, analysis and discussion.  
Instances of colloquial phrasing are rare and do not distort meaning. Referencing conventions (tables, figures, formulae) reinforce conceptual precision.  
Given the sustained, accurate use of terminology with very few slips, the work merits a 5 in the top band.

The essayâs title, abstract, and introduction work together to present **a clearly delimited spatial focus (Planning Area 2.1, Rio de Janeiro) and a precise thematic focus (comparative infrastructure inequalities between formal and informal settlements)**. Key terms such as âformal urban areasâ, âinformal areas/favelasâ, and the three service dimensions investigated are unambiguously defined early on, and each section of the report is explicitly tied back to the overarching purpose.  
â¢ Maps, hypotheses, and repeated reminders in section headings keep the reader oriented.  
â¢ Minor lapses (e.g., occasional digressions in background history) do not undermine an otherwise consistently clear communication, warranting the top band for this strand.

The essay is organised around three hypotheses that frame a **coherent argument tracing how infrastructure disparities manifest across sewage, environmental quality, and waste management**. Each body section cycles through data presentation, interpretation, and linkage back to the research question, creating a largely logical chain of reasoning.  
Counter-arguments and methodological caveats (sampling bias, safety restrictions) are acknowledged, lending balance; however, synthesis across the three themes in the final conclusion is somewhat descriptive, and some claims (e.g., direct causal links to health outcomes) are asserted rather than critically weighed.  
These qualities fit comfortably at the top of the 4-6 band; a score of 6 reflects an effective but not outstanding argument.

The analysis links quantitative results (e.g., percentages of adequate sewage access, litter counts, tree densities) with qualitative observations and literature to build explanations of infrastructure disparity. **Most conclusions are logically derived and supported by data visualisations and photographs.**  
Nonetheless, some evaluative sections generalise beyond the evidence (e.g., health implications inferred without direct morbidity data) and statistical treatment remains descriptive rather than inferential. Discussion of alternative explanations (e.g., topographical vs socio-economic factors) is present but not deeply pursued.  
Thus, the analysis is more than adequate but falls short of the sustained, fully substantiated critique required for the 7-9 band, warranting a mid-range 5 in the 4-6 band.

The report follows the IB EE conventions with **title page, abstract, extensive table of contents, logically sequenced chapters, clear sub-headings, and integrated figures/tables**. The structure consistently assists reader navigation and mirrors the three-hypothesis framework, enabling understanding.  
Transitions between sections are usually smooth, and appendices are used to hold raw data appropriately. Minor issues such as a lengthy background section and occasional redundancy do not impede comprehension.  
Overall, the structure fully supports the argument, meeting the descriptors of the upper band; 4/4 is awarded.

The student states the research question verbatim under the title and again in the introduction. **It is sharply focused, specifying service types (sewage, waste, environmental quality), contrasting urban morphologies (formal vs. informal) and a defined study area (AP 2.1).**  
The scope is realistic for an EE, and each of the three hypotheses operationalises the RQ into measurable sub-components.  
Some redundancy in wording and the late acknowledgement of accessibility limits in Rocinha show partial rather than full anticipation of feasibility issues, so the strand is strong but not perfect, meriting a solid 5 within the highest band.

annotations.comment: The WHO recommendation is cited but lacks a full bibliographic reference in 'Works Cited'. Ensure complete citation to meet academic standards.
The research question is clearly stated under the title and again in the introduction, specifying variables, contrasts, and location, which ensures a focused and measurable investigation.
The introduction uses broad terms like âinfrastructure inequalitiesâ without defining them precisely. Clarify these key concepts to strengthen the foundation of your analysis.
The background section on the origins of favelas is informative but could more directly link to the research focus and justify why these historical factors matter for your study.
Safety constraints are noted, but alternative data collection approaches are not explored. Discuss mitigation strategies to address access limitations.
Figure 7 uses a negativeâpositive scale without explaining the rationale or interpretation of negative values. Add a legend or caption clarification.
The title is presented prominently and communicates the spatial and thematic focus effectively, supporting reader orientation from the outset.
The SDG icons are wellâchosen but your text restates their purpose without deeper explanation. Expand on how specific SDG targets inform your methodological choices.
The geographical context is clear, but population and area figures from 2010 are used without critique. Discuss potential implications of relying on dated census data.
Figure 3âs caption lacks a reference to the map projection or coordinate system. Include this to enhance reproducibility and map accuracy.
The transition into Figure 4âs discussion could be smoother. Provide clearer rationale for selecting this streetâview example and its representativeness.
Figure 5âs favela boundaries map is helpful, but the legend is small and difficult to read. Increase its size or simplify symbology for clarity.
The âHypothesisâ header formatting differs from earlier section headings. Consistent styling would improve navigability and presentation.
Hypothesis 3 text is truncated midâsentence (âinformalâ). Ensure full statements are included to avoid ambiguity in your aims.
The methodology describes transect placement but lacks detail on the randomization process and sampling justification. Clarify to strengthen validity.
Survey 2 data were collected during scheduled waste pickup, biasing counts high. Reflect on how timing affects results and discuss alternative sampling times.
Tree counts rely on two random 100 mÂ² areas via Google Maps without explaining selection criteria. Outline the sampling frame to improve repeatability.
Claims about greater pipeline density in formal areas are based on visual map comparison; consider quantitative buffer or length metrics to substantiate this assertion.
The SDG 11.7 table in Figure 23 is densely formatted and hard to parse. Simplify or split it into smaller tables to improve readability.
Figure 25âs stacked bar callouts overlap chart areas and obscure data. Reposition annotations or use a legend to maintain data visibility.
Equations are presented in HTML rather than LaTeX. Rewrite as $$\frac{\text{Green Space Area}}{\text{Total Area}}\times100$$ for clarity and consistency.
The discussion of topography influencing pump station distribution is plausible but untested; incorporate elevation or gradient analysis to support this interpretation.
The discrepancy between mapped infrastructure and reported sewer access percentages is noted but not reconciled; propose a method to integrate or validate these conflicting data sources.
Figure 24âs dualâseries chart lacks clear axis labels for the accessible green space series. Add distinct axis titles or color keys to prevent misreading.
The WHO greenâspace recommendation is quoted without contextualizing its applicability to Rio de Janeiroâs urban density. Discuss local factors affecting target relevance.
Figure 28âs xâaxis label âNeighbourhoodâ is generic; list each neighborhood directly to improve immediate comprehension of the data series.
Figure 30 would benefit from an altâtext description for accessibility. Describe visual features in text to support all readers.
The assessment of street quality in Figure 32 is descriptive but could be quantified (e.g., crack counts, sidewalk width measurements) for stronger comparative analysis.
Figure 34âs photograph lacks date and location metadata in the captionâadd contextual details so the image can be properly situated in your fieldwork timeline.
